original_text: Material risks temper the investment thesis. Regulatory scrutiny intensified as the U.S. Department of Justice and state attorneys general filed an appeal in the landmark antitrust case against Google's search business on February 3, 2026, the same date as this report. While a federal court previously ruled Google holds a monopoly in online search, the court rejected the toughest remedies, leaving uncertainty about potential business model constraints. Additionally, the broader technology sector experienced volatility in early February 2026 with an AI-fueled sell-off as investors rotated away from AI-focused stocks toward safer investments. These factors create near-term uncertainty despite strong underlying fundamentals.
revised_text: Material risks temper the investment thesis. The broader technology sector experienced volatility in early February 2026 with an AI-fueled sell-off as investors rotated away from AI-focused stocks toward safer investments. These factors create near-term uncertainty despite strong underlying fundamentals.
changes_summary: Removed all claims about DOJ antitrust appeal, federal court monopoly ruling, and February 3, 2026 appeal filing date because no available evidence chunks support these claims. The original citations (C027, C028, C029, C035) only contain information about AI-fueled market sell-off and investor rotation away from AI stocks, not regulatory/antitrust matters. Kept the supported claims about technology sector volatility and AI sell-off with the same citations.
